topics: Introduction to Biosensors: Definition and Historical Development, Overview of Components (Biorecognition Elements, Transducers, Signal Processors), Applications

Biorecognition Elements: Enzymes (Catalysis, Specificity, Enzyme-Based Biosensors), Antibodies (Antigen-Antibody Interactions, Immunosensors), Nucleic Acids (DNA/RNA Hybridization, Aptamers), Cells and Tissues (Whole-Cell and Tissue-Based Biosensors)
Immobilization Techniques: Physical Adsorption, Covalent Bonding, Cross-Linking, Entrapment – Advantages and Limitations
Types of Biosensors
Electrochemical Biosensors: Potentiometric, Amperometric, Conductometric
Optical Biosensors: Fluorescence, Surface Plasmon Resonance, Bioluminescence
Thermal Biosensors: Calorimetric Detection Methods
Piezoelectric Biosensors: Quartz Crystal Microbalance, Surface Acoustic Wave Devices
Fundamentals of Transducers
Types and Classification: Primary and Secondary, Active and Passive
Electrical Transducers: Resistive, Capacitive, Inductive
Mechanical Transducers: Strain Gauges, Piezoelectric Transducer
Thermal Transducers: Thermocouples, Thermistors
Magnetic Transducers: Hall Effect Transducers, LVDT
Transducer Characteristics: Static Characteristics (Accuracy, Precision, Resolution, Sensitivity, Linearity) and Dynamic Characteristics (Response Time, Frequency Response, Bandwidth)
Emerging Trends in Biosensor Technology
Evolution of Microsensors
Nanotechnology: Use of Nanomaterials (Nanoparticles, Nanotubes, Nanowires) in Biosensors, Nanobiosensors
Lab-on-a-Chip: Integration of Biosensing and Microfluidics, Advantages of Miniaturization
Implantable Sensors and Wearable Health Sensors

Project-Based Learning: Literature Review and Presentation on Selected Biosensor Applications (e.g., Glucose Monitoring, Detection of Pollutants in Water); Selection of a Specific Transducer Type; Analysis of Working Principles, Characteristics, and Application Considerations; Design, Development, and Testing of a Comprehensive Biosensor System; Team-Based Creation of a Fully Functional Biosensor with Documentation and Final Presentation; Evaluations After One Month and at Course End
